episodes.title: Apex Defined
episodes.description: What makes a shark the ocean's apex predator — and why their role is irreplaceable.

episodes.title: The Great White Project
episodes.description: Years of tagging data on white sharks reveals migration patterns no one expected.

episodes.title: Tiger Country
episodes.description: The territorial range of tiger sharks and their role in seagrass ecosystem health.

episodes.title: Bulls in the Shallows
episodes.description: Bull sharks are the most adaptable species — and the most misunderstood.

episodes.title: Deep Hunters
episodes.description: Deep-sea shark species rarely seen by cameras are filmed for the first time.

episodes.title: Conservation Calculus
episodes.description: Shark populations are in freefall — the economics, politics, and biology of saving them.

episodes.title: New Data
episodes.description: A five-year tracking project yields landmark data on shark social behavior.

episodes.title: Nursery Watch
episodes.description: Protecting the shallow coastal nurseries where young sharks develop.

episodes.title: Finning: The Full Picture
episodes.description: A documentary look at the global shark fin trade and the campaigns against it.

episodes.title: Symbiosis
episodes.description: The unexpected relationships between sharks and the species that depend on them.

episodes.title: Thermal Shift
episodes.description: How warming ocean temperatures are changing where sharks live and feed.

episodes.title: Recovery
episodes.description: Three success stories — species pulled back from the brink through targeted protection.

extras.title: Tagging a Great White
extras.description: The full process of attaching a satellite tag to a living white shark.

extras.title: Shark Myths Debunked
extras.description: Scientists address the most persistent misconceptions about shark behavior.
